rules.title: Pre-implementation authorization verification
Multi-task cascade: authorization extends from plan to all sub-issues
Post-implementation: push then halt
Search before Q/A halt when no spec/plan found
Verify schema/API/code knowledge before claims
Spec-to-plan approval cascade
Changelog entries MUST reference actual commit/PR sources
actions: REPORT("changelog entry lacks commit/PR source reference")

rules.title: Branch-header-based categorization is primary; conventional commits are fallback
Python functions MUST NOT exceed ≈100 words
Notebook cells MUST NOT exceed ≈120 words
Source files MUST NOT exceed ≈750 words
Nesting depth MUST NOT exceed 3 levels
Extraction scan requires valid guidelines directory
Maintenance drift detection requires baseline
Concern analysis must verify against actual code
Tier 3 conflicts MUST halt for developer review
Classify before resolving — never use blanket ours/theirs
No blanket ours/theirs resolution
When in doubt classify UP
Read conflict content before resolving
No commits that silently drop committed work
Verify spec compliance after all conflicts resolved
Verification gate before drafting correspondence
Multipart/alternative format mandatory for email
Audience separation — no internal artifacts in stakeholder tier
Audience classification before drafting
Verification gate after drafting and self-review
AI byline mandatory in all correspondence
Content-type propagation — match source format
Attribution verification — no role-proximity inference
No direct implementation by orchestrator
Sub-agent dispatch required for all implementation
Stacking is prerequisite, parallelism is opportunistic
Pre-dispatch verification checkpoint mandatory
Implementation-first gate: post(assemble-work) requires deliverable
Overflow signal requires output (prevents silent halt)
Must understand before solving — read all relevant code before proposing changes
Must design before implementing — document approach and obtain approval
Must verify before declaring complete — run tests, check edge cases, validate success criteria
No scope creep — implement ONLY what is in the approved spec
All changes must be committed before branch completion
All tests must pass before branch completion
All lint must pass before branch completion
Branch must be pushed before claiming completion
Shared content blocks MUST have a master copy in .opencode/.guidelines/
Drift between master and destination MUST be resolved before sync
Registry MUST be updated after any fragment operation
Submodule provenance tracking after push or promotion
Post-creation URLs must be extracted from API response (NEVER constructed)
Pre-creation URLs must be constructed from verified session-init values with character-match
Chat output format requires all 4 elements in order
Mandatory skill dispatch before direct API calls
Byline verification before posting AI-authored content
Close issues only after PR merge confirmed
Never replace issue body with shorter content
Sub-issues go under plan, not spec
Title dedup gate before issue creation
Platform routing before all operations
Bug discovery does NOT authorize fixing
rules.source: 000-critical-rules.md §Bug Discovery Does Not Authorize Bug Fixing

rules.title: Fix spec must target root cause, not symptom
No code edits during analysis phase
rules.source: 000-critical-rules.md §Bug Discovery Does Not Authorize Bug Fixing

rules.title: Comments read before triage decision
Symptom-only fix-specs are forbidden
Fix spec still requires explicit authorization before code changes
Audit findings are internal — not posted to GitHub
ALL .ipynb access MUST use the-notebook-mcp exclusively — ZERO TOLERANCE
If the-notebook-mcp is unavailable, ALL notebook operations are FORBIDDEN — no fallback
actions: REPORT("the-notebook-mcp unavailable — no notebook operations possible")

rules.title: Notebook execution requires explicit per-session user authorization
Production data execution is ABSOLUTELY FORBIDDEN
Clean-room input must not contain existing plan details
Significant gaps must recommend brainstorming
PR requires explicit instruction — approved/go does NOT authorize PR
Base branch must be dev for feature PRs
PR body must use Summary/Outcome/Fixes format
No PR for dev-to-main (use release-promotion)
Squash verification before PR
Never merge PRs — human-only operation
Work branch guard — no individual PRs during work execution
Changelog required before PR
Pipeline scope >= for_pr authorizes PR creation
Enforced principles (KISS, DRY, SRP, Fail Fast, Defensive Programming) MUST be checked during design and review
Deliberately relaxing a principle MUST be documented with tradeoff note
YAGNI violations MUST be flagged
actions: FLAG("YAGNI violation — feature not in spec")

rules.title: TDD mandatory — no skill without failing test first
No hardcoded identity values in skill files
Verification-enforcement gate before skill generation
Worktree awareness mandatory in all skills
actions: REJECT(skill until Worktree Mode section added)

rules.title: Enforcement test step mandatory after skill creation/update
Required frontmatter fields present
Session-init variable names must match canon
No 0-based counting patterns in skill/task docs
Spec audit is mandatory for all new specs
Auto-fix findings applied directly without authorization
Conditional findings require authorization before application
Flag-for-review findings are never applied
Body preservation gate prevents issue body erasure
Ground-truth verification is mandatory for all audits
Executive summary posted to chat after every audit
Pre-spec investigation mandatory before requirements
Select-existing pathway before new spec creation
Verification-enforcement gate before spec generation
Requirements task mandatory before write
Spec must be persisted as GitHub Issue
SC scope alignment validation after write
Environment context mandatory before generation
Domain context mandatory before generation
Verification gates between sections — reasoning chain must connect
Live verification mandatory — no training knowledge fallback
Single-path rule — one method per operation
Real-values rule — no generic placeholders
Exact-match verification — no soft-passing
DNS-specific validation for DNS runbooks
Verification-enforcement gate before runbook generation
Read-only analysis mandate during diagnosis
Bug discovery does NOT authorize fixing
Hypothesis must be verified before proceeding
actions: VERIFY(via srclight_get_symbol OR srclight_get_callees OR bash)

rules.title: Fix must target root cause, not symptoms
Self-correction on unauthorized code edits
Fix requires authorization; diagnosis does not
No scope creep in fixes
Tests MUST be written before implementation code
Test MUST fail without implementation (RED phase)
actions: RECOMMEND("test does not validate anything — rewrite test")

rules.title: Design artifacts MUST be verified against spec requirements
Design artifacts MUST be toolkit-agnostic — no framework-specific markup
Implementation MUST match design artifacts from ui-design
Interaction spec is REQUIRED input — cannot implement without it
Worktree MUST be created before file modification when WORKTREE_REQUIRED is set
If worktree.fatal=1 appears, HALT immediately and report
If worktree.path is empty after creation, HALT — fatal error
Stash+checkout in main repo is FORBIDDEN when worktree mode active
No completion claim without evidence
Per-SC evidence table all PASS required
Evidence must be live-source verified
Exact comparison mode for external verifications
Content generation requires verification gate
Reporting unverified information as verified is a critical violation
Revisit pass required after generation
Sub-agent output without evidence artifacts is rejected
Plan must derive from approved spec only
actions: REPORT("spec not approved, cannot create plan")

rules.title: No placeholders in plan
Sub-issues under plan, not spec
Spec revision revokes plan approval
RED verification checkpoint mandatory per TDD step
Verification-enforcement gate before plan generation
Scope-aware plan approval cascade
Duplicate plan check before creation

tasks.bypass_violation: CRITICAL: Soliciting Authorization for Already-Authorized Phrases
CRITICAL: Inline Screening of Authorization Sets
CRITICAL: Assuming Closed Issues Are Verified
CRITICAL: Assuming Closed Issues Are Verified
CRITICAL: Bug Reports Without Fix Spec
CRITICAL: Assuming Closed Issues Are Verified
CRITICAL: Wrong Chat Output at Halt Points
CRITICAL: Skipping Completion Guarantee on Workflow Halt
code size limits exceeded on new/modified code
CRITICAL: Resolving conflicts without classification risks silently eroding committed work
CRITICAL: Skipping completion task may leave conflict resolution state unverified
Correspondence without verification — drafting without verification gate is a critical violation
Silent Agent Termination — halting without completion task is a critical violation
CRITICAL: Skipping Completion Guarantee on Workflow Halt

postconditions: all_issues_dispatched == true && work_branch_created == true && at_least_one_file_modified == true
lint_pass == true && tests_pass == true && branch_pushed == true

tasks.bypass_violation: CRITICAL: Skipping Completion Guarantee on Workflow Halt
fragment created without master or registry

postconditions: branch_deleted == true && issues_closed == true && dev_synced == true

tasks.bypass_violation: CRITICAL: Skipping Completion Guarantee on Workflow Halt
CRITICAL: Creating issues without validation bypasses duplicate detection and conflict checking
CRITICAL: Direct github_issue_write calls skip byline verification, label enforcement, and pre-creation validation
CRITICAL: Direct github_add_issue_comment calls bypass substantiveness gate and byline enforcement
CRITICAL: Closing issues before PR merge confirmed is a critical violation
CRITICAL: Multi-task plans without sub-issues are a critical violation
CRITICAL: Skipping completion task may leave mandatory steps (labels, auditors, sub-issues) unverified
CRITICAL: Gathering incomplete context leads to incorrect triage decisions
CRITICAL: Triage drives all downstream dispatch; skipping causes wrong skill invocation
CRITICAL: Bug reports without fix spec sub-issues cannot be legitimately closed
Specs should be audited but path is determined by triage, not mandatory for all issues
Q/A path is for non-bug, non-spec issues; not all issues require it
CRITICAL: Skipping completion task may leave terminal-state dispatch unverified
CRITICAL: Skipping pre-PR checklist risks un-squashed commits, missing changelog, and stale branch state
Sub-issue collection needed for work PRs but not required for single-issue branches
CRITICAL: Skipping completion task may leave PR state unverified
principles not loaded for design judgment
Skill without TDD — creating skill without failing test first violates Iron Law
Skill update without TDD — modifying skill without baseline failure test violates Iron Law
Validation recommended but not blocking — flagged issues should be addressed before skill is used
Silent Agent Termination — halting without completion task is a critical violation
CRITICAL: Skipping fidelity audit for spec documents
CRITICAL: Skipping SC precision audit for spec documents
CRITICAL: Skipping Completion Guarantee on Workflow Halt

preconditions: brainstorming exploration completed OR investigation results provided
tasks.bypass_violation: Spec Without Investigation — creating spec without completed investigation is a critical violation
Spec not persisted — spec must be GitHub Issue, not chat output
Silent Agent Termination — halting without completion task is a critical violation
Runbook without live verification — generating unverified instructions is a critical violation
Incident tracking optional but recommended for accountability
Silent Agent Termination — halting without completion task is a critical violation
CRITICAL: Skipping diagnosis leads to vibe debugging — random changes without understanding
CRITICAL: Fix without diagnosis targets symptoms, not root cause
CRITICAL: Skipping completion task may leave terminal-state dispatch unverified
implementation does not pass the test
implementation does not satisfy interaction spec
implementation fails validation against interaction spec
file modification without worktree when WORKTREE_REQUIRED
file operations target main repo instead of worktree
CRITICAL: Skipping Completion Guarantee on Workflow Halt
CRITICAL: Skipping Verification-Enforcement During Content Generation
CRITICAL: Sub-agent output lacks evidence artifacts
CRITICAL: Skipping Completion Guarantee on Workflow Halt
Implementation without plan — plan creation is mandatory before implementation
Silent Agent Termination — halting without completion task is a critical violation

gates.condition: frontmatter_name_present == true AND frontmatter_description_present == true AND frontmatter_type_present == true
finding_classification == 'auto-fix' AND fix_targets_issue_body == true AND fix_is_non_substantive == true

evidence_artifacts.name: authorization_result
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get) confirms authorization

evidence_artifacts.name: scope_resolution
evidence_artifacts.verification: scope_auto_resolve output confirms parsed scope

evidence_artifacts.name: sub_issue_count
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get_sub_issues) count matches plan

evidence_artifacts.name: work_state_file
evidence_artifacts.verification: .opencode/tmp/work-*.md exists

evidence_artifacts.name: screening_result_contract
evidence_artifacts.verification: screen-issue sub-agent returns structured YAML

evidence_artifacts.name: drift_findings_table
evidence_artifacts.verification: Each drift finding has pattern, location, severity, recommendation

evidence_artifacts.name: cross_reference_verification
evidence_artifacts.verification: Each cross-reference verified against actual skill content

evidence_artifacts.name: phase_structure_analysis
evidence_artifacts.verification: Each phase has concern, deployment independence, risk profile, blast radius

evidence_artifacts.name: boundary_verification
evidence_artifacts.verification: Each boundary claim verified via srclight against actual code

evidence_artifacts.name: coverage_findings
evidence_artifacts.verification: Each sub-issue checked for scope match against plan phase

evidence_artifacts.name: conflict_classification
evidence_artifacts.verification: bash: git diff output showing conflict markers, then classification decision recorded in chat

evidence_artifacts.name: tier2_resolution_note
evidence_artifacts.verification: Chat notification with file path, reason, and resolution side for Tier 2 conflicts

evidence_artifacts.name: tier3_developer_notification
evidence_artifacts.verification: Chat notification with feature branch intent, parent branch intent, and agent recommendation

evidence_artifacts.name: tier3_github_issue
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get) → issue with conflict-resolution label exists for complex Tier 3

evidence_artifacts.name: spec_compliance_check
evidence_artifacts.verification: Read spec body, compare against resolved files to confirm spec intent preserved

evidence_artifacts.name: audience_classification
evidence_artifacts.verification: Agent self-documentation of audience tier determination

evidence_artifacts.name: stakeholder_content_filter
evidence_artifacts.verification: Grep draft for internal artifact patterns (runbook paths, internal IPs, step numbers, CLI commands)

evidence_artifacts.name: verification_revisit_result
evidence_artifacts.verification: verification-enforcement --task revisit output confirming all markers resolved or escalated

evidence_artifacts.name: content_type_inspection
evidence_artifacts.verification: read source .eml file → grep Content-Type header

evidence_artifacts.name: branch_state
evidence_artifacts.verification: git branch --show-current confirms expected branch

evidence_artifacts.name: push_confirmation
evidence_artifacts.verification: git push output confirms branch pushed

evidence_artifacts.name: compare_url
evidence_artifacts.verification: URL format matches dev...branch pattern

evidence_artifacts.name: pr_merge_status
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_pull_request_read(method=get) merged_at != null

evidence_artifacts.name: worktree_location
evidence_artifacts.verification: git rev-parse --show-toplevel matches worktree.path

evidence_artifacts.name: byline_verification
evidence_artifacts.verification: Check body string for 'Co-authored with AI' or emoji byline before github_issue_write/github_add_issue_comment call

evidence_artifacts.name: merge_status
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_pull_request_read(method=get, pullNumber=N) → check merged field == true

evidence_artifacts.name: body_length_check
evidence_artifacts.verification: Read current body via github_issue_read(method=get), compare len(new_body) >= 0.8 * len(original_body)

evidence_artifacts.name: dedup_evidence
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_search_issues(query) → confirm no overlapping title exists

evidence_artifacts.name: sub_issue_link
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get_sub_issues, issue_number=N) → confirm link exists

evidence_artifacts.name: root_cause_section
evidence_artifacts.verification: Read fix spec body — confirm 'Root Cause' section exists and identifies underlying cause

evidence_artifacts.name: fix_spec_sub_issue_link
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get_sub_issues, issue_number=bug_report_N) → confirm fix spec linked

evidence_artifacts.name: comments_read_evidence
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get_comments, issue_number=N) → all comments retrieved

evidence_artifacts.name: authorization_status
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get_comments) → filter for authorization comments from developer

evidence_artifacts.name: clean_room_input
evidence_artifacts.verification: ./tmp/clean-room-input-N.md exists and excludes plan details

evidence_artifacts.name: comparison_findings
evidence_artifacts.verification: Each finding has level (phase/step/content), description, recommendation

evidence_artifacts.name: sub_issue_alignment
evidence_artifacts.verification: Each sub-issue checked against plan phase content

evidence_artifacts.name: working_tree_clean
evidence_artifacts.verification: bash: git status → confirm 'nothing to commit'

evidence_artifacts.name: branch_pushed
evidence_artifacts.verification: bash: git log origin/<branch>..HEAD → confirm empty

evidence_artifacts.name: squash_commit_count
evidence_artifacts.verification: bash: git log --oneline dev..HEAD | wc -l → single commit for single-issue, N for work

evidence_artifacts.name: changelog_file_exists
evidence_artifacts.verification: glob(pattern='**/CHANGELOG*') → file exists

evidence_artifacts.name: co_author_trailers
evidence_artifacts.verification: bash: git log -1 --format='%B' → check for AI and human trailers

evidence_artifacts.name: pr_url_extraction
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_create_pull_request response → html_url field (NEVER constructed from template)

evidence_artifacts.name: tdd_red_phase_evidence
evidence_artifacts.verification: bash .opencode/tests/with-test-home opencode-cli run '<test message>' → baseline failure output

evidence_artifacts.name: validation_output
evidence_artifacts.verification: uv run .opencode/skills/skill-creator/scripts/validate_skill_cards.py → REQ-1/2/3 check

evidence_artifacts.name: quick_validate_output
evidence_artifacts.verification: ./.opencode/skills/skill-creator/scripts/quick_validate.py <skill-folder> → frontmatter check

evidence_artifacts.name: placeholder_check
evidence_artifacts.verification: grep for hardcoded agent names, model IDs, developer names in SKILL.md and task/*.md files

evidence_artifacts.name: audit_findings_table
evidence_artifacts.verification: Each finding has Subtask, Problem Class, Location, Classification, Fix Action, Severity

evidence_artifacts.name: auto_fix_verification
evidence_artifacts.verification: Re-read of issue body confirms all auto-fixes applied

evidence_artifacts.name: body_length_check
evidence_artifacts.verification: len(new_body) >= 0.8 * len(original_body)

evidence_artifacts.name: ground_truth_evidence
evidence_artifacts.verification: Each metadata claim verified via tool call against actual state

evidence_artifacts.name: executive_summary_posted
evidence_artifacts.verification: Executive summary present in chat with all required fields

evidence_artifacts.name: self_review_placeholder_scan
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get) → search body for TBD/TODO/placeholder patterns

evidence_artifacts.name: spec_issue_created
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get) → check title prefix [SPEC] and needs-approval label

evidence_artifacts.name: sc_phase_mapping
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get) → verify SC-to-phase trace references exist

evidence_artifacts.name: live_verification_per_claim
evidence_artifacts.verification: bash --help output, webfetch vendor docs, glob reference data files

evidence_artifacts.name: verification_gap_block
evidence_artifacts.verification: YAML block with sources_attempted, claims_unconfirmed, user_action_required

evidence_artifacts.name: verification_results_table
evidence_artifacts.verification: Row-by-row exact comparison: each field independently PASS or FAIL

evidence_artifacts.name: hypothesis_verification
evidence_artifacts.verification: srclight_get_symbol/srclight_get_callees/bash command confirming hypothesis matches live code behavior

evidence_artifacts.name: root_cause_identified
evidence_artifacts.verification: Bug report issue body contains 'Root Cause' section with identified cause

evidence_artifacts.name: fix_minimal_verification
evidence_artifacts.verification: srclight_get_dependents → check blast radius; uv run pytest → confirm pass

evidence_artifacts.name: bug_report_issue
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get, issue_number=N) → confirm bug report exists

evidence_artifacts.name: section_evidence_table
evidence_artifacts.verification: Each content section has evidence entries with Claim, Domain, Source, Verified fields

evidence_artifacts.name: unverified_markers_resolved
evidence_artifacts.verification: No ⚠️ UNVERIFIED markers remain after revisit pass

evidence_artifacts.name: audience_classification
evidence_artifacts.verification: Content audience tier (stakeholder/operator) is recorded before generation

evidence_artifacts.name: spec_approved_verification
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get_labels) + github_issue_read(method=get_comments) → confirm approval

evidence_artifacts.name: plan_issue_created
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get) → check title prefix [PLAN] or combined plan section

evidence_artifacts.name: sub_issue_linkage
evidence_artifacts.verification: github_issue_read(method=get_sub_issues, issue_number=plan_N) → count matches phase count
